21.correct: The Sangeet Natak Akademi granted Sattriya the status of a classical dance form in 2000.

wrong.A: 1980 is too early for this recognition.
wrong.B: 1990 is not the year Sattriya was recognised.

21.fact: Sattriya, from Assam's Vaishnavite monasteries (Satras), is one of India's eight classical dance forms.

22.correct: The Ramayana narrates the story connected with Dussehra (Vijayadashami) - Lord Rama's victory over the demon king Ravana.

wrong.A: The Mahabharata is a different epic, not the basis of Dussehra's central story.
wrong.C: The Bhagavad Gita is part of the Mahabharata, not the Dussehra-Ravana story.
wrong.D: The Vedas are hymns and rituals, not this narrative.

22.fact: Dussehra marks the triumph of good over evil; effigies of Ravana are burnt on this day.

23.correct: In 2025, senior Congress leader Shashi Tharoor led India's diplomatic outreach delegation related to Operation Sindoor to countries including the USA and Panama.

wrong.A: Rahul Gandhi did not lead this particular delegation.
wrong.B: P. Chidambaram did not lead it.
wrong.D: Sonia Gandhi did not lead the delegation.

23.fact: India sent multi-party delegations abroad to explain its position after Operation Sindoor.

24.correct: Excess nutrients in water bodies cause eutrophication, which leads to algal blooms and oxygen depletion.

wrong.A: Global warming raises temperatures but is not the cause of nutrient enrichment.
wrong.B: Fog is unrelated to water-body nutrient levels.
wrong.C: Acid rain lowers pH; it is not the nutrient-enrichment process.

24.fact: Fertiliser and sewage runoff are the main causes of eutrophication.

25.correct: In 2025, Shweta Menon was elected President of the Association of Malayalam Movie Artists (AMMA), a milestone for women's leadership in the body.

wrong.A: Ansiba Hassan was not elected president.
wrong.B: Kukku Parameswaran is not the elected president.
wrong.C: Lakshmipriya is not the elected president.

25.fact: AMMA is the influential professional body of Malayalam cinema actors.

26.correct: The Indian Peninsular Plateau is composed mainly of ancient igneous and metamorphic rocks, making it one of the oldest and most stable landmasses.

wrong.A: It is not chiefly made of sedimentary 'layered' rocks.
wrong.C: Volcanic (Deccan Trap) rocks cover part of it, but the plateau as a whole is igneous and metamorphic.

26.fact: The peninsular plateau is part of the ancient Gondwana landmass.

27.correct: Rani Rudramadevi of the Kakatiya dynasty changed her name on her inscriptions and was portrayed as a man (Rudradeva) to rule more easily in a patriarchal era.

wrong.A: Kittur Chennamma was a 19th-century queen who fought the British, not known for this.
wrong.C: Chand Bibi defended Ahmednagar against the Mughals.
wrong.D: Rani Durgavati of Gondwana fought the Mughals.

27.fact: Rudramadevi was one of the few women to rule a major South Indian kingdom in her own right.

28.correct: The Jharkhand government launched the 'Mukhyamantri Maiya Samman Yojana' in August 2024, providing financial assistance to eligible women.

wrong.B: Haryana did not launch this scheme.

28.fact: Several states have introduced direct cash-transfer schemes for women.

29.correct: In April 2025 the Ministry of New and Renewable Energy launched the 'Green Hydrogen Certification Scheme of India' (GHCI).

wrong.A: The Ministry of Petroleum and Natural Gas did not launch it.
wrong.C: The Ministry of Environment did not launch it.
wrong.D: The Ministry of Power was not the launching ministry.

29.fact: Green hydrogen is produced using renewable energy, and certification verifies its 'green' credentials.

30.correct: In February 2025, Union Minister Dr Jitendra Singh launched 'Srijanam', India's first indigenous automated biomedical-waste treatment plant.

wrong.A: Narendra Modi did not launch this plant.
wrong.C: Rajnath Singh did not launch it.
wrong.D: Manoj Sinha did not launch it.

30.fact: Proper biomedical-waste treatment prevents the spread of infection from hospital waste.

31.correct: As of around August 2025, NASA had confirmed roughly 6,000 exoplanets (planets outside our solar system).
31.fact: Most confirmed exoplanets were discovered by the Kepler and TESS space telescopes.

32.correct: The Chandrayaan-1 mission ended in 2009 after contact with the spacecraft was lost.

wrong.B: 2013 is the year Mangalyaan was launched, not when Chandrayaan-1 ended.

32.fact: Despite ending early, Chandrayaan-1 confirmed the presence of water molecules on the Moon.

33.correct: In February 2025, a 5G laboratory developed at a university was inaugurated at Guwahati (IIT Guwahati region).

wrong.C: Mumbai was not the location cited.

33.fact: 5G test labs help researchers and startups develop and validate next-generation telecom applications.

34.correct: The S-400 missile-defence (air-defence) system is made by Russia.

wrong.B: The USA makes systems like the Patriot/THAAD, not the S-400.
wrong.C: China operates the S-400 (imported) but did not build it.
wrong.D: Germany did not build the S-400.

34.fact: India signed a deal to buy S-400 systems from Russia for long-range air defence.

35.correct: The Shanghai Cooperation Organisation (SCO) was established at Shanghai (China) in 2001.

wrong.A: It was not founded in New Delhi.
wrong.B: It was not founded in Tehran.
wrong.C: It was not founded in Islamabad.

35.fact: The SCO grew out of the 'Shanghai Five'; India and Pakistan joined as full members in 2017.

36.correct: The 2012 Summer Olympic Games were held in London.
36.fact: London has hosted the Summer Olympics three times - 1908, 1948 and 2012.

37.correct: The 3000 m steeplechase is held inside the stadium (on the track), unlike the marathon and the long-distance race-walks, which are conducted outside the stadium on roads.

wrong.A: The marathon is run on roads outside the stadium.
wrong.C: The 50 km walk is held outside the stadium.
wrong.D: The 20 km walk is held outside the stadium.

37.fact: The steeplechase includes fixed barriers and a water jump on the track.

38.correct: High-power appliances such as geysers and air coolers use a 15 A current rating (and a corresponding socket), unlike low-power devices that use 5 A.

wrong.A: 4 A is too low for high-power appliances.
wrong.B: 8 A is not the standard high-power rating.
wrong.C: 11 A is not the standard household high-power rating.

38.fact: Indian sockets commonly come in 5 A (light loads) and 15/16 A (heavy loads) ratings.

39.correct: 'Hurkiya Baul' is a traditional folk song form of Uttarakhand.

wrong.D: It is not from West Bengal (which has its own 'Baul' tradition, distinct from Hurkiya Baul).

39.fact: It is named after the 'hurka', a small hourglass-shaped drum used in Uttarakhand folk music.

40.correct: Both Cerium (I) and Europium (II) ARE rare-earth elements, so neither of them is a 'non-rare-earth' element - hence 'Neither I nor II'.

wrong.A: 'Only I' is wrong because Cerium is a rare-earth element.
wrong.B: 'Only II' is wrong because Europium is a rare-earth element.
wrong.C: 'Both I and II' would mean both are not rare-earth, which is incorrect.

40.fact: The rare-earth elements are the 15 lanthanides plus scandium and yttrium.
